title: Weak Symmetric Encryption Algorithm
description: DES, 3DES, RC2, RC4, and AES-ECB are broken or insufficiently secure for protecting sensitive data (OWASP A02:2021 / CWE-327). These algorithms are vulnerable to various cryptanalytic attacks.

finding.message: Weak cipher algorithm '{{token}}' in {{file}} at line {{line}}.

references: Use AES-256-GCM for authenticated encryption; it provides both confidentiality and integrity.
ChaCha20-Poly1305 is a good alternative, especially in contexts without AES hardware acceleration.
RFC 7465 prohibits RC4 in TLS. 3DES has a 64-bit block size making it vulnerable to Sweet32.
NIST SP 800-175B Revision 1 provides approved cryptographic algorithm guidance.
